question: The concept of 'counter-mapping' in critical geography
options: The Eastern Caribbean arc was formed by subduction of the North American Plate under the Caribbean Plate
Indigenous and marginalised communities creating their own maps to assert land rights and spatial identities
Water is not just a natural resource but is shaped by and shapes social, political and economic relations
The global economy is divided into core, semi-periphery and periphery — structurally unequal zones
answer: Indigenous and marginalised communities creating their own maps to assert land rights and spatial identities

options: The Eastern Caribbean arc was formed by subduction of the North American Plate under the Caribbean Plate
